expectations.text: Defends the heatmap or recommends small-multiples cohort retention curves (not a 3D surface, not a pie chart, not a single averaged line)
expectations.evidence: 'Ship this instead: small-multiple retention curves, grouped by quarter. 8 panels (one per signup quarter, 2024 Q1 â†’ 2025 Q4), arranged 2Ã—4 or 4Ã—2.'

expectations.text: Explicitly addresses how to handle the triangular shape / missing cells (greyed out, NaN, diagonal cutoff)
expectations.evidence: 'The triangular-data problem vanishes: recent cohorts just have shorter curves. No empty cells, no awkward void.'

expectations.text: Reframes 'polish' as data presentation craft (typography, spacing, color scale) rather than chartjunk/decoration
expectations.evidence: 'Your designer gets something that looks composed rather than computed.' Plus advice on identical axes, direct labels, no rainbow, no 3-D, no gradient fills, and explicit Y-axis scaling to avoid Lie-Factor.

expectations.text: Specifies a sensible color scale for retention â€” sequential or diverging anchored on a target, NOT rainbow
expectations.evidence: 'No rainbow colormap on the heatmap if your designer insists on keeping it â€” viridis or a single-hue sequential at minimum'

expectations.text: Provides at least one concrete actionable next step (revised chart description, code, or design spec)
expectations.evidence: Detailed design spec: '8 panels... X = months since signup (0â€“12), Y = % retained. Each panel shows the 3 monthly cohorts in that quarter as thin gray lines, with the quarter's average overlaid darker.' Plus a table spec with column headers.
